description: Design a multi-region active-passive architecture for Contoso's global e-commerce storefront with automatic failover. Primary region East US 2, secondary West US 3. Target RTO < 5 minutes, RPO < 30 seconds for transactional data. Stateless API tier with Azure Front Door for global traffic management and health-probe-based failover.
constraints: RPO < 30 seconds for order and payment data (Azure SQL auto-failover group with zone-redundant replicas)
RTO < 5 minutes measured from health probe failure to traffic cutover completion
Session state must not be region-affine — use Azure Cache for Redis with geo-replication
requiredCapabilities: Azure Front Door Premium with health probes and priority-based routing
Azure SQL auto-failover group with readable secondary
Cosmos DB multi-region writes for product catalog (eventual consistency acceptable for reads)
Automated DR drill runbook executable quarterly
assumptions: 80% of traffic originates from North America; secondary region serves as warm standby, not active load sharing
Static assets are globally cached via Azure CDN and do not require regional failover
inlineRequirements: Document manual failover procedure for planned maintenance, including DNS TTL considerations and cache warming

documents.name: region-selection-rationale.txt
documents.content: Fictional rationale: East US 2 chosen for proximity to data center; West US 3 for Azure availability zone support and regulatory redundancy.

topologyHints: Stateless API tier behind Azure Front Door — no server affinity
Use Azure Traffic Manager as fallback if Front Door itself experiences an outage (edge case)
securityBaselineHints: Encrypt cross-region replication channels (Azure SQL failover groups use TLS by default)
Ensure Key Vault secrets are replicated or accessible from both regions
